howItWorks.intro: The calculator determines how many plants fit in a rectangular area based on the spacing pattern and any edge buffer.
rules: Square grid: plants in evenly spaced rows and columns
Triangular offset: alternating rows are shifted by half the spacing, fitting ~15% more plants
Edge buffer reduces the usable planting area from each side
howItWorks.outro: Triangular (offset) spacing is more efficient and gives each plant equal distance from all neighbors. It is ideal for groundcovers, perennial beds, and dense plantings.

faq.question: What is triangular spacing?
faq.answer: In triangular (or offset/staggered) spacing, every other row is shifted by half the plant spacing. This creates equilateral triangles between plants, giving each plant equal distance from all its neighbors and fitting about 15% more plants per area.

faq.question: How do I know what spacing to use?
faq.answer: Check the plant tag or seed packet for the recommended spacing. This is the mature spread of the plant. Common examples: 12" for most perennials, 18-24" for shrubs, 6" for groundcovers, and 36" for large shrubs.

faq.question: Should I leave space at the edge?
faq.answer: Yes. A 3-6 inch buffer from the bed edge keeps plants from hanging over borders and allows air circulation. For raised beds with walls, half the plant spacing as a buffer ensures plants don't crowd the edge.

faq.question: How do I convert between spacing and plants per square foot?
faq.answer: For square spacing: plants per sq ft = 144 ÷ (spacing in inches)². For 12" spacing: 144/144 = 1 plant per sq ft. For 6" spacing: 144/36 = 4 plants per sq ft.

faq.question: Is the Plant Spacing Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Plant Spacing Calculator?
sections.body: The Plant Spacing Calculator is a free online tool designed for gardeners and growers who need quick, accurate calculations in the gardening and horticulture space. By entering your bed length, bed width, plant spacing, you get instant results including total plants, plants per sq ft, rows. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total plants right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In gardening and horticulture, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by gardeners and growers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to grow a thriving garden with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your bed length and need to find the right total plants. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Bed Length (feet)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your bed length — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: bed width, plant spacing, spacing pattern, edge buffer. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total plants, plants per sq ft, rows. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your bed length before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total plants and plants per sq ft — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to grow a thriving garden.
If you're unsure about your edge buffer, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
